message:
perf(core): inline LENGTH/UPPER/SUBSTR scalar functions to eliminate eval_scalar_fn dispatch overhead (bd-wwqen.6 B6)

Replace the dynamic string-name lookup through eval_scalar_fn() with
direct Rust functions for the three string projection variants used in
the PreparedSimpleStringProjection fast path:

- direct_length(): returns char count for Text, byte count for Blob,
  text representation length for Integer/Float, NULL for NULL — matching
  SQLite LENGTH() semantics exactly

- direct_upper(): ASCII uppercasing after coercing non-NULL values to
  text via to_text(), returning NULL for NULL inputs

- direct_substr(): full SQLite SUBSTR() semantics including 1-based
  indexing, negative start (counts from end), optional length parameter,
  and negative length (backwards extraction). Each edge case (start=0
  mapped to position 0, NULL propagation, empty-string on out-of-range)
  follows the SQLite documentation precisely.

The previous code called eval_scalar_fn("length"/etc.) which performed a
HashMap lookup on every row to find the registered function, then
dispatched through a trait-object call. For string-heavy queries this
lookup+dispatch dominated the hot loop. The new inline functions are
monomorphized and branch-predicted, eliminating both the hash probe and
the indirect call.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
